hydross.SCORECARD_FINDING: HPS (563) landed well below the Top 100 average (1,269), and overheal (67.9%) exceeds the sample's single worst parse (58%) - both real gaps, though this kill had zero deaths, so the inefficiency reflects overhealing rather than a survivability problem.
hydross.SPELL_COMPOSITION_FINDING: Lifebloom's share ran 11.5 points ahead of the Top 100 average, a real but middling composition gap for this raid night.
hydross.COOLDOWN_FINDING: Every tracked cooldown here (Innervate, Nature's Swiftness, 3 Swiftmends) was used in line with the Top 100 sample - no real deviation on this kill.
hydross.TARGET_FINDING: Coverage (98.7% vs. 83.3% average, +15.4) ran well above the Top 100 norm, with top-target concentration (54.7% vs. 47.9% average) tracking close to typical - healing reached nearly the entire raid on this kill.
hydross.MANA_TIMING_FINDING: Mana stayed out of real danger all kill (only 0.0% of the fight under 20%), ending around 18.1%.

lurker.SCORECARD_FINDING: HPS (646) ran well below the Top 100 average (1,605), and overheal (53.8%) exceeds the sample's single worst parse (49.1%) - with 3 real deaths logged, this is one of the lower-percentile kills of the raid night (29th).
lurker.SPELL_COMPOSITION_FINDING: Lifebloom's share ran 18.9 points ahead of the Top 100 average, one of the larger composition gaps of the raid night.
lurker.COOLDOWN_FINDING: Rebirth was cast on Captinspanky, a real overcast against the Top 100 sample (only 16% of parses use it here) - though the cast landed about a minute after Captinspanky's own death, a delayed but real response. Vajony and Hatchpeppers's deaths weren't directly answered by any cooldown.
lurker.TARGET_FINDING: Coverage (72.3% vs. 56.8% average, +15.5) ran well above the Top 100 norm, with 14 distinct targets touched - top-target concentration (28.3% vs. 25.3% average) tracked close to typical.
lurker.MANA_TIMING_FINDING: Mana stayed out of real danger all kill (only 0.0% of the fight under 20%), ending around 31.7%.

leotheras.SCORECARD_FINDING: HPS (699) ran well below the Top 100 average (1,517), and overheal (66.7%) exceeds the sample's single worst parse (54.3%) - with 8 real deaths logged (the most of any boss this raid night, including two players who died twice each), the below-average HPS likely reflects a chaotic, high-death fight rather than a healing-output problem in isolation.
leotheras.SPELL_COMPOSITION_FINDING: Regrowth's share ran 32.4 points behind the Top 100 average - the largest single composition gap of the raid night, in the opposite direction from Danceswtrees's more common pattern of running ahead of the benchmark on other kills.
leotheras.COOLDOWN_FINDING: Rebirth was cast on Captinspanky about 30 seconds after Captinspanky's first death - a real, if delayed, response - but Captinspanky died a second time later in the fight with no further cooldown available. None of the other 6 deaths (including a second death for Destromental and Churbert) show a cooldown landing close enough afterward to call a real save; the closest timing (Hez, within 110 milliseconds of a Swiftmend) still wasn't enough.
leotheras.TARGET_FINDING: Coverage (69.7% vs. 51% average, +18.7) ran well above the Top 100 norm, with 19 distinct targets touched - consistent with a chaotic, high-death fight spreading healing across most of the raid.
leotheras.MANA_TIMING_FINDING: Mana stayed out of real danger all kill (only 14.4% of the fight under 20%), ending around 16.1%.

karathress.SCORECARD_FINDING: HPS (1,144) ran below the Top 100 average (1,834), but the more telling number is active time (80.6% vs. 99.2% average) - a real, unusually large gap. Danceswtrees died mid-fight (one of 3 real deaths this kill), directly explaining the reduced activity: once Danceswtrees is dead, there's no more healing to log for the rest of the encounter.
karathress.SPELL_COMPOSITION_FINDING: Lifebloom's share ran 21.9 points ahead of the Top 100 average, one of the larger composition gaps of the raid night - consistent with a kill cut short by the self-death above.
karathress.COOLDOWN_FINDING: Rebirth was never cast despite 3 real deaths this kill, including Shlicktree and Deepbowlz, who both died before Danceswtrees's own death and could plausibly have been answered. Mana Potion also went uncast against a 51%-used norm.
karathress.TARGET_FINDING: Coverage (82% vs. 74% average) and top-target concentration (42.3% vs. 34.5% average) both ran modestly above the Top 100 norm - typical for a kill with real raid damage spread across 16 distinct targets.
karathress.MANA_TIMING_FINDING: Mana stayed out of real danger all kill (only 0.0% of the fight under 20%), ending around 48.2%. Spent 21.3% of the kill sitting above 90% mana, room to have healed more aggressively.

morogrim.SCORECARD_FINDING: HPS (891) ran below the Top 100 average (1,442), and overheal (57.4%) exceeds the sample's single worst parse (51.1%) - the 64th percentile here comes on the raid's successful attempt, after a real wipe on this boss earlier in the night (see the raid overview).
morogrim.SPELL_COMPOSITION_FINDING: Regrowth's share ran 21.9 points behind the Top 100 average, a real undercast - the opposite direction from Danceswtrees's more common pattern of running ahead on Lifebloom.
morogrim.COOLDOWN_FINDING: Nature's Swiftness went uncast against an 86%-used Top 100 norm - the one real cooldown gap on this kill. Innervate, Swiftmend, and Mana Potion were all used in line with the sample.
morogrim.TARGET_FINDING: Top-target concentration (26.5% vs. 38.5% average) ran below the Top 100 norm, while coverage (64.6% vs. 67.4% average) tracked close to typical - healing was spread more evenly across the raid than the benchmark usually shows.
morogrim.MANA_TIMING_FINDING: Mana stayed out of real danger all kill (only 5.9% of the fight under 20%), ending around 39.9%.

vashj.SCORECARD_FINDING: HPS (665) ran below the Top 100 average (1,139), and overheal (63.2%) exceeds the sample's single worst parse (53.5%) - on the raid's longest kill this night (498 seconds), with 5 real deaths logged.
vashj.SPELL_COMPOSITION_FINDING: Lifebloom's bloom-effect share ran a modest 6.9 points behind the Top 100 average - the smallest composition gap of the raid night, tracking close to the benchmark.
vashj.COOLDOWN_FINDING: Rebirth was cast on Deepbowlz about 36 seconds after Deepbowlz's death - a real, if delayed, response. Purehentai, Destromental, and Vinnyvoss all died within the same rough window but weren't directly answered; Captinspanky died much later in the fight, unanswered by any cooldown. Mana Potion was used 4 times, consistent with a long, mana-intensive encounter.
vashj.TARGET_FINDING: Coverage (62.5% vs. 50.7% average, +11.8) ran above the Top 100 norm, with 25 distinct targets touched - the widest spread of the raid night, consistent with a long fight and real raid-wide damage.
vashj.MANA_TIMING_FINDING: Mana stayed comfortable for most of the kill (only 8.8% of the fight under 20%) but was driven down to 10.9% by the end - a late squeeze, not a sustained one.

alar.SCORECARD_FINDING: HPS (569) ran well below the Top 100 average (1,075), and overheal (69.2%) exceeds the sample's single worst parse (61.1%) - yet Danceswtrees still ranked 1st of 4 tracked healers this kill on both real comparison metrics (iLvl Healing Rank and Raw Healing Rank), suggesting the whole healing team ran conservative here rather than an individual issue. Zero deaths, so the overheal reflects genuine excess healing, not a fight cut short.
alar.SPELL_COMPOSITION_FINDING: Lifebloom's share ran 16.8 points ahead of the Top 100 average - a real but middling gap for this raid night.
alar.COOLDOWN_FINDING: Every tracked cooldown here (Innervate, Nature's Swiftness, 2 Swiftmends, 2 Mana Potions) was used in line with the Top 100 sample - no real deviation on this kill.
alar.TARGET_FINDING: Coverage (95.2% vs. 78.9% average, +16.3) ran well above the Top 100 norm, with top-target concentration (49.1% vs. 43.5% average) tracking close to typical - healing reached nearly the whole raid on this kill.
alar.MANA_TIMING_FINDING: Mana stayed comfortable for most of the kill (only 0.3% of the fight under 20%) but was driven down to 14.3% by the end - a late squeeze, not a sustained one.

voidreaver.SCORECARD_FINDING: HPS (1,241) ran below the Top 100 average (2,458), and overheal (38.7%) exceeds the sample's single worst parse (33%) - with 6 real deaths logged, a rough kill broadly, not an isolated healing issue.
voidreaver.SPELL_COMPOSITION_FINDING: Lifebloom's share ran 29 points ahead of the Top 100 average - the largest composition gap of the raid night.
voidreaver.COOLDOWN_FINDING: No cooldown deviated from the Top 100 sample here, but none of the 6 real deaths show a genuine save either - the closest timings (Nature's Swiftness and Mana Potion, both self-targeted) are coincidental proximity, not a cooldown actually answering someone else's death.
voidreaver.TARGET_FINDING: Top-target concentration (26.4% vs. 9.4% average, +17) ran well above the Top 100 norm - a real, notable gap - while coverage (63.5% vs. 37.2% average) also ran well ahead of typical.
voidreaver.MANA_TIMING_FINDING: Spent 49.1% of this kill below 20% mana (44 casts made that low), a real efficiency squeeze rather than a one-off dip.

solarian.SCORECARD_FINDING: HPS (644) ran below the Top 100 average (1,225), yet this kill landed at the 83rd percentile - the highest of the raid night. Zero deaths and overheal (52%) squarely within the Top 100 range (28.2%-65.4%) point to a clean, low-crisis kill rather than a standout healing performance.
solarian.SPELL_COMPOSITION_FINDING: Regrowth's share ran 30.9 points ahead of the Top 100 average - a real, sizeable overcast, and the opposite pattern from Danceswtrees's usual Lifebloom-heavy composition on other kills this raid.
solarian.COOLDOWN_FINDING: Every tracked cooldown (Innervate, Nature's Swiftness, Swiftmend, Mana Potion) went uncast against real Top 100 usage rates of 61-90% - a consistent, real pattern across the board, plausible on a clean, no-crisis kill where no emergency response was ever needed.
solarian.TARGET_FINDING: Coverage (49.4% vs. 52.8% average) and top-target concentration (14.7% vs. 16.4% average) both tracked close to the Top 100 norm - a fairly typical target-distribution profile despite the unusually low cooldown usage.
solarian.MANA_TIMING_FINDING: Mana stayed out of real danger all kill (only 0.0% of the fight under 20%), ending around 30.9%. Spent 20.6% of the kill sitting above 90% mana, room to have healed more aggressively.

kaelthas.SCORECARD_FINDING: HPS (477) ran well below the Top 100 average (1,014), and overheal (72.2%) is the highest of any boss tonight, exceeding the sample's single worst parse (61.7%). With 4 real deaths logged, the 22nd percentile - the lowest of the raid night - reflects a rough kill more broadly, not an isolated healing issue.
kaelthas.SPELL_COMPOSITION_FINDING: Lifebloom's share ran 16.8 points ahead of the Top 100 average - a real but middling gap, matching Alar's exact same-sized gap earlier in the raid.
kaelthas.COOLDOWN_FINDING: Nature's Swiftness went uncast against an 88%-used Top 100 norm - the one real cooldown gap. Rebirth was cast on Sackajaweeba about 17 seconds after Sackajaweeba's death, a real if delayed response; Banepain's death came just 564 milliseconds after a Swiftmend cast, close enough to suggest the heal landed but wasn't enough. Deepbowlz and Vinnyvoss, who died at the same moment later in the fight, weren't directly answered.
kaelthas.TARGET_FINDING: Coverage (79.9% vs. 58.4% average, +21.5) ran well above the Top 100 norm - the widest coverage gap of the raid night - with 19 distinct targets touched, consistent with a difficult, high-death kill.
kaelthas.MANA_TIMING_FINDING: Mana stayed out of real danger all kill (only 0.8% of the fight under 20%), ending around 38.0%.

RaidOverview.GEAR_CONSISTENCY_FINDING: consistent, aside from two likely-benign single-kill gear swaps (MainHand, OffHand)
RaidOverview.GEAR_FINDING_NOTE: One real gear gap stands out: the Back slot (item 28765) carries no permanent enchant across every kill this raid night - a cheap, static enchant that would apply immediately and doesn't require any fight-specific loadout choice.
RaidOverview.RAID_SUMMARY_FINDING: Kael'thas Sunstrider (22nd percentile), The Lurker Below (29th), and Hydross the Unstable (34th) are this raid night's lowest-percentile kills and the clearest priorities for a full breakdown. Leotheras the Blind (8 real deaths, the most of the raid, including two players who died twice) and Void Reaver (6 deaths) point to survivability issues beyond a single rotation gap. Morogrim Tidewalker also had a real wipe before the successful kill shown here. Every percentile shown here is matched to this exact report and fight ID, not a duplicate-upload fallback.
RaidOverview.RAID_WARNING_BANNER: Overheal exceeded the Top 100 sample's single worst parse on 8 of 10 kills this raid night. This is the single most consistent pattern across the whole raid - see each boss's own page for the real numbers, and consider whether a rotation or target-priority adjustment could recover some of this as effective throughput.

GearCheckItems.Description: MainHand and OffHand vary on a minority of kills
GearCheckItems.LongDetail: Both slots differ from the baseline loadout on exactly one kill each, not a lasting swap - consistent with a fight-specific choice rather than a gearing gap.

GearCheckItems.Description: Elixir of Healing Power (Battle) and Elixir of Draenic Wisdom (Guardian) both active on all 10 kills, plus Well Fed food buff
GearCheckItems.LongDetail: A real, complete consumable setup all raid night - the Battle + Guardian Elixir pairing is a fully valid alternative to a Flask under the real TBC rule.
